title: cacalia pyramidale
intro: Cacalia pyramidale, also known as the Chinese artichoke or artichaut de Chine, is a perennial plant native to China and parts of the Himalayas. It is valued for its edible tubers, which are rich in vitamins and minerals, and its leaves, stems, and roots, which possess medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. Cacalia pyramidale is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its tubers can be eaten raw or cooked, offering a range of health benefits, including improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
